channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran says nuclear enrichment rights cannot be ‘negotiated’
item.description: Iran’s nuclear enrichment is a right that ‘already exists’, foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said.

item.title: Russian drone hits Chinese ship off Ukraine before Putin visits Xi Jinping
item.description: Ukraine says its territory is attacked overnight with 524 drones and 22 missiles.

item.title: Israeli forces begin intercepting Gaza-bound aid flotilla near Cyprus
item.description: Israeli forces have begun intercepting boats from the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla.

item.title: Migrants ‘feared for their lives’ as Libyan gunmen fired on rescue ship
item.description: The Libyan coastguard threatened those on board Sea-Watch 5, but it's the NGO ship's captain who is under investigation.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship docks in the Netherlands
item.description: MV Hondius was carrying 25 crew members and two medical personnel as it reached the Dutch port of Rotterdam.

item.title: How war affects civilians for generations
item.description: War can have a hidden legacy that no one talks about.

item.title: Video: US calls on G7 to impose US-led sanctions on Iran
item.description: The US Treasury Secretary says he’s calling ‘the world’ to join the US in imposing sanctions on Iran.

item.title: US military carries out more strikes against ISIL fighters in Nigeria
item.description: Attacks conducted days after US and Nigeria say they killed deputy ISIL leader.

item.title: Israeli settlers run over livestock in occupied West Bank
item.description: Israeli settlers drove into a herd of livestock belonging to a Palestinian shepherd in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Israel built two military bases in Iraq before war on Iran: New York Times
item.description: Israeli forces had been preparing the makeshift sites in western Iraq since late 2024, the US newspaper reported.

item.title: Why was Neymar angry at his substitution and will he play in the World Cup?
item.description: Brazil's leading goal scorer was left fuming at a substitution days before their World Cup squad announcement.

item.title: Mitchell, Cavaliers eliminate Pistons, will play Knicks in East finals
item.description: Led by Donovan Mitchell's 26 points, Cleveland dominate Detroit in Game 7 to advance to the NBA's final four.

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel controls 60 percent of Gaza
item.description: Netanyahu says Israel controls 60 percent of Gaza

item.title: ‘Hindu order runs India’: Court declares another medieval mosque a temple
item.description: The Madhya Pradesh High Court rules that the historic Kamal Maula mosque is a temple dedicated to a Hindu goddess.

item.title: Al Jazeera reports from Hormuz Island as Iran says it controls the strait
item.description: Al Jazeera has been given exclusive access to Iran’s Hormuz Island.

item.title: Israeli forces storm Gaza-bound aid flotilla off Cyprus
item.description: Organisers of the flotilla say the vessels were boarded in international waters, as Turkey condemns 'piracy'

item.title: Mali drone strikes kill at least 10 civilians at wedding
item.description: Mali's military carries out strikes after attacks last month by al-Qaeda-linked fighters and Tuareg separatists.

item.title: Iran war day 80: Trump issues warning; Tehran ‘ready’ to confront attacks
item.description: The fear of renewed US strikes in Iran looms while Israeli attacks continue in Lebanon despite extended 'ceasefire'.

item.title: Earthquake hits southern China, toppling buildings
item.description: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake has struck southern China, causing over a dozen buildings to collapse, killing at least two.

item.title: Britain faces weeks of leadership limbo in slow-motion coup against Starmer
item.description: Keir Starmer battles to retain his authority, critics say he's a 'lame-duck' leader.

item.title: Growing bread queues in Gaza as Israel restricts fuel, flour imports
item.description: Bread shortages deepen in Gaza as flour imports dwindle and bakeries struggle to meet rising demand amid fuel scarcity.

item.title: Sinner wins Italian Open to complete career Golden Masters
item.description: The first Italian to win the title in 50 years enters the French Open as favourite with Alcaraz out injured.

item.title: Former UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting announces bid to replace Starmer
item.description: Former UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting has announced he will run against Prime Minister Keir Starmer as Labour leader

item.title: When are Hajj and Eid al-Adha 2026? A visual guide to the Muslim pilgrimage
item.description: Nearly 2 million Muslims will embark on the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca, which begins on May 25.

item.title: Uganda responds with emergency measures to Ebola outbreak
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Catherine Soi reports on the emergency measures implemented in Uganda in response to the Ebola outbreak.
